fine_dining.name: Bottiglieria 1881
fine_dining.atmosphere: Intimate vaulted cellar with an extensive wine list
fine_dining.booking_tips: Book well in advance, the dining room is small and fills quickly
fine_dining.local_tips: Opt for the tasting menu with wine pairings to experience Kraków's only Michelin-starred kitchen

fine_dining.name: Pod Nosem
fine_dining.atmosphere: Elegant townhouse dining beneath Wawel Castle
fine_dining.booking_tips: Reserve a table near the windows for views toward Wawel
fine_dining.local_tips: Their take on the Kraków-style maczanka pork sandwich is a local highlight

fine_dining.name: Szara Gęś
fine_dining.atmosphere: Refined dining under Gothic vaults on the Main Market Square
fine_dining.booking_tips: Request a table in the vaulted main hall for the best atmosphere
fine_dining.local_tips: Dine right on Europe's largest medieval market square overlooking the Cloth Hall

fine_dining.name: Pod Aniołami
fine_dining.booking_tips: Book ahead for a candlelit table in the historic cellar
fine_dining.local_tips: Try the meats grilled over beechwood embers for a true old-Kraków experience

casual_dining.name: Miód Malina
casual_dining.atmosphere: Warm, colorful interior with a cozy Polish-country feel
casual_dining.booking_tips: Popular with visitors, reserve ahead for evening dining
casual_dining.local_tips: The name means 'honey raspberry'; their hearty pierogi and żurek are crowd favorites

casual_dining.name: Hawełka
casual_dining.atmosphere: Historic restaurant on the Main Market Square since 1876
casual_dining.booking_tips: Arrive early for lunch to secure a table during peak season
casual_dining.local_tips: A Kraków institution serving classic Polish dishes for well over a century

casual_dining.name: Restauracja Pod Wawelem
casual_dining.atmosphere: Bustling beer-hall style restaurant with large portions
casual_dining.booking_tips: Come hungry; portions are generous and shared platters are popular
casual_dining.local_tips: Famous for huge portions of hearty Polish fare and beer poured by the half-litre

casual_dining.name: Marchewka z Groszkiem
casual_dining.atmosphere: Homely, retro interior evoking a Polish grandmother's kitchen
casual_dining.booking_tips: A favorite in Kazimierz, arrive early to avoid the lunch rush
casual_dining.local_tips: The name means 'carrot with peas'; great affordable comfort food off the tourist trail

casual_dining.name: Hamsa
casual_dining.atmosphere: Bright, relaxed hummus-and-mezze spot in the former Jewish quarter
casual_dining.booking_tips: Book ahead on weekends; it sits on a lively Kazimierz square
casual_dining.local_tips: A modern take on Israeli cuisine fitting Kazimierz's Jewish heritage

casual_dining.name: Starka
casual_dining.atmosphere: Elegant yet cozy restaurant known for its vodka cellar
casual_dining.booking_tips: Reserve ahead and ask about their flavored vodka flights
casual_dining.local_tips: Named after the aged Polish rye vodka; pair their house infusions with hearty Polish dishes

street_food.name: Obwarzanek Street Carts
street_food.location: Rynek Główny and across the Old Town
street_food.local_tips: The blue carts sell Kraków's protected pretzel-ring; eat it fresh in the morning

street_food.name: Endzior at Plac Nowy
street_food.local_tips: The round market hall on Plac Nowy is the home of the zapiekanka; a late-night Kraków staple

street_food.name: Stary Kleparz Market Food Stalls
street_food.location: Stary Kleparz, north of the Old Town
street_food.local_tips: Kraków's historic market; try grilled oscypek sheep cheese with cranberry

street_food.name: Christmas Market on Rynek Główny
street_food.local_tips: Visit the December market for hot grzaniec and grilled highland cheese

coffee_shops.name: Café Camelot
coffee_shops.atmosphere: Quaint, folk-art filled café on a quiet Old Town corner
coffee_shops.food_options: Cakes, breakfasts, and light Polish dishes
coffee_shops.local_tips: Tucked just off the Main Square, beloved for its apple cake and cozy nooks

coffee_shops.name: Charlotte
coffee_shops.atmosphere: Bright, airy bakery-bistro on Plac Szczepański
coffee_shops.food_options: Bakery breakfasts, tartines, and light meals
coffee_shops.local_tips: Arrive early for warm croissants; the square-side terrace is a local favorite

coffee_shops.name: Cytat Café
coffee_shops.atmosphere: Relaxed, literary-themed café ideal for lingering
coffee_shops.local_tips: A quiet spot to read and sip coffee away from the busy square

coffee_shops.name: Mleczarnia
coffee_shops.atmosphere: Vintage interior with a leafy summer courtyard
coffee_shops.local_tips: The shaded garden across the street is one of Kazimierz's loveliest hideaways

coffee_shops.name: Forum Przestrzenie
coffee_shops.specialty: Riverside café in a former hotel
coffee_shops.atmosphere: Retro-industrial space in the modernist Hotel Forum building
coffee_shops.local_tips: Lounge on deckchairs by the Vistula with views across to Wawel

bars.name: Alchemia
bars.atmosphere: Dark, candlelit warren of rooms with a bohemian vibe
bars.local_tips: A Kazimierz icon on Plac Nowy with frequent live concerts in the back room

bars.name: Wódka Café Bar
bars.atmosphere: Tiny, lively bar dedicated to Polish vodka
bars.local_tips: Order a flight of infused vodkas served ice-cold the traditional Polish way

bars.name: Multi Qulti
bars.atmosphere: Relaxed bar showcasing Poland's craft brewing scene
bars.local_tips: Ask the staff for recommendations from the rotating selection of Polish craft beers

bars.name: Singer Café
bars.atmosphere: Dimly lit Kazimierz bar with vintage Singer sewing-machine tables
bars.local_tips: Grab a candlelit table by the window for classic Kazimierz nightlife

bars.name: Hala Forum Beer Garden
bars.atmosphere: Open-air riverside lounge with deckchairs and views of Wawel
bars.local_tips: Perfect for a sunset drink on the Vistula boulevards in summer

food_tours.name: Kraków Old Town Food Tour
food_tours.focus: Traditional Polish cuisine and street food
food_tours.booking_tips: Book at least a week in advance, especially for weekend tours

food_tours.name: Kazimierz Food and Heritage Tour
food_tours.focus: Jewish quarter food culture and zapiekanka
food_tours.booking_tips: Evening tours include the lively Plac Nowy zapiekanka stalls

food_tours.name: Kraków Market and Vodka Tour
food_tours.booking_tips: Morning start for market freshness, book well in advance

cooking_classes.name: Polish Pierogi Workshop
cooking_classes.booking_tips: Reserve your spot at least two weeks in advance

cooking_classes.name: Traditional Kraków Cooking Class
cooking_classes.booking_tips: Check for seasonal menus featuring market ingredients

cooking_classes.name: Polish Vodka and Food Pairing Class
cooking_classes.booking_tips: Must be 18+, book in advance for weekend sessions

markets.name: Stary Kleparz
markets.local_tips: Kraków's oldest market, operating for centuries; best for fresh local produce

markets.name: Plac Nowy Market
markets.type: Square market and street food hub
markets.local_tips: The round hall is the spiritual home of the zapiekanka, busiest at night

markets.name: Hala Targowa
markets.location: Grzegórzki, east of the Old Town
markets.local_tips: Come Sunday morning for the sprawling flea and antiques market

spring: Żurek with white sausage for Easter
Fresh spring vegetables and young greens
winter: Christmas market roasted oscypek and sausages

local_specialties.description: Kraków's protected ring-shaped bread, boiled then baked and topped with salt, sesame, or poppy seeds; sold from blue street carts.
Polish dumplings filled with potato and cheese (ruskie), meat, sauerkraut and mushroom, or sweet fruit; boiled or pan-fried.
Open-faced toasted baguette topped with mushrooms, cheese, and garlic sauce; the iconic street food of Plac Nowy in Kazimierz.
Sour rye soup with white sausage and egg, often served in a hollowed-out bread bowl.
Hunter's stew of sauerkraut, fresh cabbage, and assorted meats slow-cooked for hours; a Polish winter staple.
Smoked sheep's-milk cheese from the Tatra highlands, often grilled and served with cranberry sauce.
A Kraków specialty of slow-braised pork in a savory caraway gravy served in a soft roll for dipping.
Crispy fried potato pancakes, served savory with sour cream or goulash, or sweet with sugar.

drinks.name: Polish vodka
drinks.description: Poland's national spirit, made from rye or potato; sipped ice-cold straight or as flavored infusions like Żubrówka.

drinks.name: Polish craft beer
drinks.description: A thriving craft scene of IPAs, lagers, and seasonal ales served at multitap bars across Kraków.

drinks.name: Kompot
drinks.description: Traditional homemade fruit drink of stewed seasonal fruits, served warm or chilled as a non-alcoholic accompaniment to meals.

drinks.name: Grzaniec
drinks.description: Polish mulled wine spiced with cloves and cinnamon, a winter and Christmas-market favorite (grzane wino).

drinks.name: Starka
drinks.description: Aged Polish rye vodka mellowed in oak barrels, smooth and amber-hued, traditionally served as a sipping spirit.

drinks.name: Żubrówka
drinks.description: Bison grass vodka with a subtle herbal, vanilla note, often mixed with apple juice in a 'szarlotka' cocktail.

drinks.name: Krupnik
drinks.description: Sweet Polish honey-spiced liqueur, traditionally served warm in winter as a comforting digestif.

drinks.name: Polish mineral water
drinks.description: Sparkling and still mineral waters from Polish springs, a common table drink alongside Kraków meals.
